# Sub-Requirement-Level Mapping Decomposition

**Date:** 2026-05-31
**Goal:** Bring the framework crosswalks from coarse article/control level to **sub-requirement granularity**, where relationships become precise (the coarse "overstated equivalents" resolve into genuine sub-level equivalents or honest subsets) and the ISO 27001 inheritance hub is maximised.

---

## Result

**32 crosswalks ¬∑ 4 845 sub-level mapping rows.** Generated by Sonnet decomposer agents (~5√ó cheaper than Opus; the whole program ‚âà 2.4M agent tokens across 6 fan-out workflows).

### Relationship distribution
| relationship | rows | note |
|---|---|---|
| **equivalent** | 2 228 (46 %) | at sub-level nearly half are genuine 1:1 ‚Äî the predicted "matches much better in detail" |
| subset | 1 072 | a sub-control implements part of a broader target |
| superset | 440 | source broader (e.g. national gold-plating, cloud additions) |
| partial_overlap | 683 | |
| related | 321 | |
| additive | 61 | new controls with no predecessor (MRIS delta, ISO/C5 new-edition controls) |
| n/a (LED) | 40 | BDSG Teil-3 law-enforcement sections ‚Äî flagged non-GDPR |

53 rows carry `UNVERIFIED-` targets (paid-standard sub-clauses, mostly ISO 27005 ‚Äî kritis_iso27005 34, nis2_iso27005 14, mris 4, iso27701-versions 1). The importer auto-skips these; they need a manual cross-check against the purchased standards.

---

## Coverage (32 crosswalks)

**ISO 27001 hub satellites** (max data-reuse inheritance): iso27701 (196), bsi-c5:2020 (231) + :2026 (1240), eucs (145, near-full equivalence), iso27017 (102), iso27018 (99), iso22301 (52), tisax (127), bsi-grundschutz (494, official BSI Zuordnungstabelle), nist-csf (189, NIST OLIR), cis-v8 (160), soc2 (105).
**Privacy/DP cluster** (legal-risk): gdpr‚Üîiso27701 (140), bdsg‚Üîgdpr (118), gdpr‚Üîiso27018 (73), iso27018‚Üîiso27701 (57), tisax-dp‚Üîiso27701 (54), tisax-dp‚Üîbdsg (43).
**EU regulatory core:** nis2‚Üîiso27001 (110), dora‚Üîiso27001 (104), ai-act‚Üîiso42001 (96), nis2‚Üînis2umsucg (57).
**Risk + continuity:** dora‚Üîiso27005 (110) / iso22301 (51), nis2‚Üîiso27005 (41) / iso22301 (44), kritis‚Üîiso27005 (80) / iso22301 (71).
**Version deltas:** iso27001 2013‚Üí2022 (125, 11 additive), iso27701 2019‚Üí2025 (97), bsi-c5 2020‚Üí2026 (175).
**App-specific:** mris‚Üîiso27001 (59 ‚Äî **all additive/superset, 0 equivalent ‚Üí confirms MRIS is a pure 27001 extension**).

### Findings surfaced during decomposition
- **GDPR‚Üî27701/27018** coarse "equivalents" were over-claimed; sub-level shows them as subset, plus real defects (wrong target clauses, a non-existent 6.13.2, missing Art.22/26 mappings ‚Äî all corrected in the decomposition).
- **BDSG**: ¬ß45-84 (Teil 3) are Law-Enforcement-Directive, NOT GDPR ‚Äî flagged `legal_basis: LED-2016/680` (the coarse CSV mis-attributed them to GDPR).
- **NIS2UmsuCG**: German gold-plating identified (BSIG ¬ß31/¬ß33/¬ß39 have no NIS2 equivalent ‚Üí superset).
- **EUCS / TISAX / C5** ‚Üî ISO 27001: very high genuine equivalence (shared control lineage) ‚Üí strong inheritance value.

---

## App integration

`SeedSubRequirementsCommand` + `ImportSubMappingsCommand` + `SubRequirementResolver` (committed `d850b7f5a`, 11 tests):
- Seeds each sub-ID as a `ComplianceRequirement` (`requirementType='sub_requirement'`, `parentRequirement` always set ‚Äî hierarchy integrity).
- Imports decomposition rows as `ComplianceMapping` (relationship‚Üípercentage: equivalent=100/superset=90/subset=75/partial=60/related=40), **`lifecycleState='draft'`** (review gate ‚Äî legal relevance, no auto-publish).
- Global catalogue (no tenant_id), idempotent, audit-logged, UNVERIFIED/LED/`n/a` placeholders skipped.

Verified live run seeded 878 sub-requirements + 707 draft mappings from the first 8 crosswalks with 0 orphans; the remaining crosswalks seed identically on demand.

---

## Open follow-ups
- Resolve the 53 `UNVERIFIED-` sub-clauses against purchased ISO 27005 / MRIS source.
- Specialist review of the `draft` mappings before publishing (especially the legal-risk privacy ones).
- Optional next decomposers: TKG‚Üî22301/27005, NIS2‚ÜîDORA lex-specialis, ISA/IEC 62443 (deferred ‚Äî niche/limited match). BaFin-BAIT deferred per request.
- Seed sub-requirements into per-tenant catalogues if the product surfaces them in tenant UIs (currently global catalogue).
